title: Puutarhan parhaat palat – Kesän maksuttomat opastukset Kaisaniemessä
content:
On a guided tour, you’ll journey through millions of years of evolution along the paths of the Evolution Garden, and immerse yourself in a world of scents and colours in the Garden of the senses. You’ll also admire unique lichen and moss gardens, while hearing stories about the garden’s past and present, and the buildings found in the area.

Tours will take place from June 23 to August 22, 2026. Free entrance, no prior registration required.

  • Tuesdays, Fridays and Saturdays at 15 (3 pm)

    • The tour starts at the greenhouse entrance and lasts approximately 40 minutes.

    Kaisaniemenranta 2, Helsinki

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

A temporary bridge for pedestrians will be built on the east side of Arkadiankatu over the Baana. The aim is to maintain the pedestrian route on the east side of the bridge until the temporary bridge is operational. Cyclists will also be able to use the bridge.

The Baana route underneath the bridge is expected to remain open for most of the renovation's duration, with some minor exceptions.

There will be changes to the motor vehicle routes by the bridge and around it in January. If possible, should be favoured during the bridge renovation.

Connections to the bus terminals in Kamppi will remain available, but there will be a slight diversion of routes.

Trams 1 and 1T will run via Simonkatu and Kamppi. Lasipalatsi and the Natural History Museum will not be served.

The diversion is due to the renovation of the Arkadiankatu bridge and will remain in place until late 2026. The renovation will not affect HSL's bus services. 

Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13

News

  • Finnish Museum of Natural History

    • Finnish Museum of Natural History

title: Ukrainan sota - yliopiston suositukset
content:
The University of Helsinki has condemned the military action by Russia and expressed its support for Ukraine and its university community. We have also expressed our support for Ukraine, together with other Finnish universities, through statements by and the . As a demonstration of support, we have illuminated buildings in the colours of the Ukrainian flag and raised the flag on our premises.

One of the principles of the University of Helsinki is to promote international collaboration in research and higher education, and to support these efforts even in difficult circumstances. Russia’s indefensible military aggression in Ukraine has required exceptional measures, which include those taken in the collaboration between universities.

The University of Helsinki is monitoring both national and EU-level sanctions, adapting our policies and processes accordingly. Together with other Finnish and international universities, the University of Helsinki strives to promote the end of military action in Ukraine, while supporting Ukrainian institutions and people.
snippet: The University of Helsinki has condemned the military action by Russia and expressed its support for Ukraine and its university community. We have also expressed our support for Ukraine, together with
creator: terojuut@helsinki.fi

title: “We all just want to feel seen, heard, and belong” – Una Europa Staff Week etsi tapoja katsoa yliopistoa uusin silmin
content:
Una Europa Staff Week, hosted by the University of Helsinki, invited 40 participants from across Europe – students, professors, and administrative staff – to reimagine the university as a common space for everyone, where learning can happen beyond classrooms.

We often think that learning happens inside a classroom, between teachers and students. Students go to a lecture, take notes, go home, study from books, memorize, write essays, and learn how to pass exams. But is learning just about reading, analysing and criticising knowledge, and preparing students to graduate? 

Universities as social institutions are often overlooked as communities that shape wider societies. Today’s societies are built around inequalities and isolation. With hierarchies between academics, professional staff and students, we live in our own silos and realities – building our identities and, in the end, reinforcing these societal hierarchies. As Kritika Sharma, a student from University College Dublin, notes:
snippet: Una Europa Staff Week, hosted by the University of Helsinki, invited 40 participants from across Europe – students, professors, and administrative staff – to reimagine the university as a common space
creator: karokuus@helsinki.fi

title: LECI Research Seminar on April 22nd: The impact of the use of digital tools outside school
content:
In this seminar, senior researcher Aleksejs Šņitņikovs from Rīga Stradiņš University will give a short talk regarding the upcoming Horizon call titled "The impact of the use of digital tools outside school and for communication on educational outcomes and mental health” (HORIZON-CL2-2026-01-TRANSFO-04) and he is especially keen on finding potential collaborators. He will give a short talk on his current plan for the project and how his earlier work on the topic ties in with it.

Shortly about the project:

In this project, we will develop a research methodology and conduct a study to address the questions outlined in the call, such as:

* Which types of digital device use do pupils perform during school and do their digital activities differ depending on different contexts (i.e., during class, during breaks or between classes)?

* How can digital leisure activities contribute to skills development, such as problem-solving and digital literacy and what is the transferability of these skills into education?

* To what extent is the impact of digital tools for leisure on academic performance mediated by students’ well-being?
snippet: In this seminar, senior researcher Aleksejs Šņitņikovs from Rīga Stradiņš University will give a short talk regarding the upcoming Horizon call titled "The impact of the use of digital tools outside
creator: jaaskosa@helsinki.fi

title: LECI Research Seminar on February 6th: Flourishing, Purpose and Meaning in Education
content:
In this seminar, professor Hannu Heikkinen, professor Kirsi Tirri and postdoctoral researcher Lari Launonen present their research, followed by a joint discussion. The seminar focuses on the interrelated concepts of flourishing, meaningfulness, and purpose — concepts that are increasingly used in discussions about the aims of education. What do these concepts mean? How do they relate to each other? And what should education look like if it aims to promote flourishing, meaningfulness, or purpose?

10.15-10.30 Professor Hannu L. T. Heikkinen (Finnish Institute for Educational Research, University of Jyväskylä)

Flourishing as the Aim of Education: Hopping on and off the Bandwagon

creator: jaaskosa@helsinki.fi

title: The teacher of field courses guides students into practical work life
content:
After graduating from Wageningen University with a master’s degree in food technology, I started working in the food industry for the large Nordic dairy cooperative Arla Foods. Over the course of more than ten years, I have worked in various roles across different sectors of the food industry. During these years I gained valuable experience how projects are executed, how teams and businesses operate, how challenges are addressed, and how strategic investments are made. Since 2024, I had the opportunity to share these experiences as a Lecturer at the Satakunta University of Applied Sciences, where I contributed to several project-based courses that connect students directly with real-world challenges. 

At the core of my approach is a drive to make tomorrow better than today, bringing theory into practice and work together as a team to achieve greater results.

title: Marlon Tobaben väittelee aiheesta Tietosuoja datatehokkaassa syväoppimisessa
content:
M.Sc. Marlon Tobaben defends his PhD thesis “Privacy in Data-Efficient Deep Learning” on Friday the 26th of June 2026 at 12 in the University of Helsinki Physicum building, Auditorium E204 (Gustaf Hällströmin katu 2, 2nd floor). His opponent is Tenure Track Faculty Franziska Boenisch (CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security, Germany) and custos Professor Antti Honkela (University of Helsinki). The defence will be held in English.

The thesis of Marlon Tobaben is a part of research done in the Department of Computer Science and in the Trustworthy Machine Learning group at the University of Helsinki. His supervisor has been Professor Antti Honkela (University of Helsinki).
